tradeIdeas.title: Long Brent Crude / Long ASEAN Energy Producers
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent fell -5.5% to ~$98.30 in Asia session Mar 25 (AFP/BSS confirmed) as US peace plan + Iran's partial Hormuz opening (France24, Al Jazeera) drove the largest single-session de-escalation move since Trump's pause. Israel launched new Tehran strikes the same day; Iran has not formally responded. Tail risk dropped to 30% but Iranian rejection of peace plan after Mar 28 would reverse the move. PTTEP and Medco remain the cleanest ASEAN equity beta; use the dip below $100 as a re-entry. Reduce conviction only on confirmed ceasefire with full Hormuz commercial reopening.

tradeIdeas.title: Rebuild ASEAN Energy Long / Underweight ASEAN Banks
tradeIdeas.rationale: Brent -5.5% to ~$98.30 (AFP/BSS, Mar 25 Asia) on US peace plan + partial Hormuz opening (France24, Al Jazeera); ASEAN Energy Equities est. -3.4% tracking oil. Tactical dip below $100 is a re-entry signal — Iran has not formally responded to peace plan; Israel continues striking Tehran. iTraxx Asia IG est. ~168bp (-4bp). Energy-over-banks pair remains valid: add energy on the $98 dip, reduce only when Iran formally accepts peace plan. Trim banks underweight only if credit spreads break below 155bp on confirmed ceasefire.

tradeIdeas.title: PHP Short — Dip Opportunity on Trump-Driven Rally
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/PHP 59.75 (Yahoo Finance, Mar 24 confirmed; PHP strengthened on peace plan/Hormuz news). Brent ~$98.30 and JKM $20.53/MMBtu (Investing.com) — Philippines LNG import costs still crisis-elevated. Iran has not formally accepted US 15-point peace plan; Israel continues striking Tehran. PHP 59.50-59.75 is the tactical re-entry zone for shorts; next stress target 61.50-62.50 if peace plan rejected after Mar 28. Stop only on confirmed ceasefire with Ras Laffan restart.

tradeIdeas.title: IDR Short — Re-Entry on Trump Pause Dip
tradeIdeas.rationale: USD/IDR 16,977 (Yahoo Finance, Mar 24 confirmed; IDR strengthened on peace plan/Hormuz news). Brent ~$98.30 still $28.30/bbl above Indonesia's $70 ICP budget assumption; ID 10Y est. 6.85%, Bank Indonesia constrained. Iran has not formally accepted US 15-point peace plan; Israel continues striking Tehran. IDR 16,800-17,000 is the tactical re-entry zone for shorts; stress target 18,000 if peace plan rejected after Mar 28.

tradeIdeas.title: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG CDS Protection — Hold Through Mar 28
tradeIdeas.rationale: iTraxx Asia ex-Japan IG est. ~168bp on Mar 25 (-4bp on peace plan/Hormuz news); ASEAN HY est. ~510bp (-15bp). US 15-point peace plan sent to Iran (NYT/CNBC, Mar 25); Iran has not formally responded; Israel continues striking Tehran; 3,000 US troops (82nd Airborne) deploying to region (WSJ). Partial Hormuz opening does not resolve P&I insurance blackout or Qatar Ras Laffan shutdown. 185-200bp remains the next stress target if Iran rejects peace plan after Mar 28. Hold CDS protection through formal Iranian response.
instruments: Sapura Energy / Hyflux bond short via CDS

tradeIdeas.title: Long JKM LNG vs Short TTF — Hold Through Mar 28 Deadline
tradeIdeas.rationale: JKM $20.53/MMBtu (Investing.com, Mar 24 settle, -5.4%); TTF ~€55.00/MWh (Trading Economics, Mar 24, -10.6%). Iran opened Hormuz to non-hostile vessels (France24, Mar 25) but Qatar Ras Laffan restart remains suspended indefinitely — structural Asian LNG deficit unchanged. TTF has priced in more de-escalation than JKM. US peace plan not yet accepted; if Iran rejects after Mar 28, JKM spikes back above $23.40 Platts high while TTF response would be muted. Hold JKM-over-TTF spread long; add on Iranian rejection signal.
instruments: Asia LNG spot cargo purchase via intermediary

cfTriggers.description: CRITICAL: Sapura Energy's refinancing window remains structurally closed. iTraxx Asia IG est. ~168bp; ASEAN HY est. ~510bp — unsecured market inaccessible. Iran opened Hormuz to non-hostile vessels (Mar 25) but P&I insurance blackout remains; Iranian formal response to US peace plan due Mar 28. If rejected, full tail scenario (oil $160+) resumes. Activate secured bank lines, private credit, and DFI-backed structures immediately — do not wait for Mar 28.

PTTEP should pre-fund before THB window closes — TH 10Y est. 2.02%, USD/THB 32.663 (Investing.com, Mar 24 confirmed; THB strengthened). Brent ~$98.30 (AFP/BSS) still boosts upstream EBITDA vs $70 pre-shock; JKM $20.53/MMBtu (Investing.com) keeps gas import costs elevated. Iran opened Hormuz to non-hostile vessels (Mar 25) — positive for PTTEP Gulf JV exposure — but Iranian acceptance of US peace plan unconfirmed. Execute H2 2026 prefunding and run Gulf JV sanctions/compliance review before Mar 28.

Expand USD revolving-credit capacity immediately. Brent ~$98.30 and JKM $20.53 (Investing.com) remain structurally elevated vs pre-shock; USD/SGD 1.2731 (Yahoo Finance, Mar 24 confirmed). Iran opened Hormuz to non-hostile vessels (Mar 25) — partial positive for freight-route risk — but P&I insurance blackout and Qatar Ras Laffan shutdown persist. Narrow execution window before Mar 28 Iranian response — execute now to lock in current spread levels.

Hyflux must activate restructuring immediately. Base 20%, stress 50%, tail 30%; Brent ~$98.30 (AFP/BSS); ASEAN HY est. ~510bp. JKM $20.53 (Investing.com) still spikes gas-fired desalination costs. Iran opened Hormuz to non-hostile vessels but formal peace plan response not yet given; tail scenario (oil $160+, LNG markets seized) remains 30% probability if Mar 28 deadline expires with rejection. Unsecured market closed; pursue private-credit and quasi-sovereign structures before Mar 28.
